nodes.title: Use a shared index for worker coordination
nodes.why: Each runner grabs the next item index, so workers stay busy without duplicating work.
nodes.alternative: Pre-split items into fixed chunks per worker.
nodes.tradeoff: Simple dynamic load balancing, but relies on shared mutable state.

nodes.title: Write output back to original item index
nodes.why: Saving by `i` preserves input order even when async tasks finish out of order.
nodes.tradeoff: Deterministic output order, with a preallocated results array.

nodes.title: Branch behavior by strategy
nodes.why: This supports two useful modes: fail immediately, or collect all outcomes.
nodes.tradeoff: More flexible API, with extra branching logic.

nodes.title: Refill each runner recursively

anchor.snippet: return run(); // refill this slot with the next item

nodes.why: After finishing one task, the same runner immediately picks up the next item.
nodes.alternative: Use a `while` loop inside each runner.
nodes.tradeoff: Compact control flow, though recursion style can be less familiar.

nodes.title: Spawn exactly `k` concurrent runners

anchor.snippet: const runners = Array.from({ length: k }, () => run());

nodes.why: This caps concurrency and keeps at most `limit` tasks in flight.
nodes.alternative: Start one runner per item and throttle manually.
nodes.tradeoff: Clear concurrency boundary, with a small runner setup step.

nodes.title: Resolve final result based on strategy
nodes.why: `Promise.all` gives fail-fast semantics, while `Promise.allSettled` waits for all runners.
nodes.tradeoff: Strategy-specific behavior is explicit, with two completion paths.
